name: debugpilot
description: MCP server exposing VS Code Debug Adapter Protocol to AI agents

debugPilot.consoleBufferSize.description: Maximum number of debug console messages to buffer

debugPilot.variableDepthLimit.description: Default depth limit for variable expansion

debugPilot.sourceContextLines.description: Number of source lines to show above and below the current line

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with DebugPilot
walkthroughs.description: Learn how to connect your AI agent to VS Code's debugger via MCP.

steps.title: Install DebugPilot
steps.description:
DebugPilot is now installed. It starts an MCP server automatically when VS Code launches.

You should see a **DebugPilot :PORT** indicator in the status bar.

[Show Connection Info](command:debugpilot.showConnectionInfo)

steps.title: Configure Your MCP Client
Start a Debug Session
steps.description:
Start any debug session in VS Code — Node.js, Python, C++, or any other debugger.

Set a breakpoint in your code and launch the debugger.

[Open Run and Debug](command:workbench.action.debug.start)

steps.title: Connect Your AI Agent
steps.description:
Once your MCP client is configured, your AI agent can discover DebugPilot's tools automatically.

Ask your agent to list debug sessions — it will use the `debug_sessions` tool to see active sessions.

[Show Status](command:debugPilot.showStatus)

steps.title: Use Debug Tools
steps.description:
Your AI agent now has access to powerful debug tools:

- **debug_state** — View current location, source, locals, and call stack
- **debug_variables** — Inspect and expand variables
- **debug_evaluate** — Evaluate expressions in the current frame
- **debug_breakpoint_set** — Set breakpoints programmatically
- **debug_step** — Step over, into, or out
- **debug_continue** — Resume execution

Ask your agent to investigate a bug — it can read variables, set breakpoints, and step through code.
